steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: c = cans at \$1.94 each, t = bags of treats at \$2.49 each. The shopper can buy the items only if the total cost stays within the \$25 budget, so total cost ≤ 25. The correct inequality assigns the right price to each variable and uses ≤.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — $1.94c + 2.49t \leq 25$: \$1.94 per can times c cans plus \$2.49 per bag times t bags, total at most \$25. ✓
  • Choice B — $1.94c + 2.49t \geq 25$: correct coefficients but wrong direction — ≥ would mean the shopper must spend at least \$25, not stay under it. ✗
  • Choice C — $2.49c + 1.94t \leq 25$: swaps the prices — assigns \$2.49 to cans and \$1.94 to bags, opposite of the given information. ✗
  • Choice D — $2.49c + 1.94t \geq 25$: wrong coefficients and wrong inequality direction. ✗
That is the correct answer.
